 {"id":"frankenredis-icsm","title":"EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking","description":"# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1zxx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-bxlo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-icsm","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gess","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ie3f","title":"redis parity: CONFIG GET/SET/RESETSTAT/REWRITE should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks CONFIG GET, CONFIG SET, CONFIG RESETSTAT, and CONFIG REWRITE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/config-get.json, config-set.json, config-resetstat.json, and config-rewrite.json, but config_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:10363 serves all four subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection while preserving existing arity and subcommand validation order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:07:16.387435544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:13:26.624349627Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:13:26.622405788Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankenredis-iniy","title":"Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations","description":"Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-iniy","title":"Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations","description":"Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z","created_by":"cod-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:01.841789756Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:01.839885261Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fr-store","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-irk8","title":"EPIC: End-to-end integration test suite proving FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in","description":"# Integration Testing Epic

## Why
Unit tests (1,076) and conformance fixtures (4,055 cases) test internal logic. TCP e2e tests (5) prove basic connectivity. But there is no test that:
- Runs a real workload through frankenredis and verifies all results
- Compares frankenredis output against legacy Redis output for the same workload
- Tests multi-client concurrent access
- Tests persistence round-trip (start → write → stop → restart → verify)
- Tests pub/sub across multiple TCP connections

These integration tests are the proof that FrankenRedis is a "drop-in replacement."

## What Success Looks Like
- A test suite that starts frankenredis, connects via redis-cli or TCP, runs realistic workloads, and verifies all responses
- A differential test that runs the same commands against both frankenredis and legacy redis, comparing outputs
- Tests cover: concurrent clients, pub/sub cross-client, persistence restart, blocking commands with timeout, Lua scripting via TCP","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.896335079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.672075914Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:31.671896515Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: persistence restart (e1e9), pub/sub cross-client (v7zc), multi-client concurrency (06pn), WATCH/EXEC transactions (6axy), redis-cli smoke test (42df). 25+ E2E tests now prove FrankenRedis works as a real Redis drop-in.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-06pn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.436452385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-42df","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.828280032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-6axy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.963579323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-e1e9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.699590804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-irk8","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-v7zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:49.569119622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ix1","title":"Normalize sorted-set zero scores to avoid -0 regressions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:27:04.284868059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.689735969Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:30:12.687284050Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-iz1j","title":"Implement replication backlog buffer that stores command bytes for partial resync","description":"# Replication Backlog Buffer

## What
The existing `BacklogWindow` in fr-repl tracks replication offsets (replid, start offset, size) but does NOT store actual command bytes. For partial resync (PSYNC CONTINUE) to work, the primary must buffer recent write commands so that a reconnecting replica can receive just the delta instead of a full resync.

## Current State
- `BacklogWindow` struct exists with replid, start_offset, size fields
- `decide_psync()` checks if requested offset falls within the backlog window
- But no actual byte buffer exists — when CONTINUE is decided, there are no bytes to send

## Implementation
1. Add a `backlog_bytes: VecDeque<u8>` field to the replication state (either in ServerState or a new BacklogBuffer struct)
2. Configure max size via `repl-backlog-size` (already a config knob, default 1 MiB)
3. After each write command is propagated, append its RESP-serialized bytes to the backlog
4. When backlog exceeds max size, drain from the front (oldest bytes)
5. Track the byte offset corresponding to the front of the buffer
6. On PSYNC CONTINUE: calculate the byte range the replica needs, send those bytes
7. On PSYNC FULLRESYNC: reset backlog after snapshot send

## Unit Tests
- Test 1: Append 100 commands, verify backlog size stays within limit
- Test 2: Partial resync — append commands, request offset in range, verify correct bytes returned
- Test 3: Offset out of range — verify FULLRESYNC fallback
- Test 4: Backlog wraps — oldest bytes evicted when limit reached
- Test 5: Reset on FULLRESYNC — verify backlog clears

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need connection before backlog matters
- Must be done BEFORE command propagation (1ng7) — propagation writes to this buffer

## Files
- crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs (backlog buffer struct + methods)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (wire backlog into server state)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:15:36.338674922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.005179144Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:13.004893794Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: aof_records Vec<AofRecord> in ServerState stores command bytes, encoded_aof_stream_from_offset() extracts delta for partial resync, PSYNC CONTINUE handler at main.rs:1642-1644 sends backlog bytes to reconnecting replicas.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.676226414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
